rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Tomorrow you please come to my house.

rows.speaker: B
rows.roman: bartiini, aadre naanu baroovaga niivu maneeli iralla.
rows.english: I will come, but you won't be at home when I come.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Tomorrow when you come, I will definitely be at home.

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: Will you be at home tomorrow at 8'o clock ?

rows.speaker: A
rows.roman: ninne niivu manege hoodaaga estu gante ?
rows.english: What was the time when you went home yesterday ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.roman: ninne naanu manege hoodaaga hattu gante.
rows.english: It was 10'o clock when I went home yesterday.

rows.speaker: A
rows.roman: niivu manege hoodaaga nimma sneehitru iddraa ?
rows.english: Was your friend at home when you went ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.roman: illa naanu hoodaaga avru aafisge hoogiddru.
rows.english: No. when I went. he had gone to the office.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: When did you see him again ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: I saw him when I went in the evening.

rows.speaker: A
rows.roman: ivattu kannada klaasge yaake tadavaagi bandri ?
rows.english: Why did you come late to the kannada class to-day ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.roman: ayyoo, haalu bassee siglilla. yaavaagaluu ondalla ondu goolu.
rows.english: Oh ! I could not get the wretched bus ; always there is some trouble or the other

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Was it late when you left ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.roman: haudu, naanu eeluvaaglee eelu gante, aadrinda tadavaaytu.
rows.english: When I woke up, it was already seven, therefore I was delayed.
